Urrigon Greyjoy
Aeron Greyjoy (left) and Urrigon (right) by Wodania
Alias Urri[1]
Allegiance House Greyjoy
Culture ironborn
Born 268–270 AC[2]
Pyke[3]

Pyke

Mother Lady Sunderly
Books
  • A Feast for Crows (mentioned)
  • The Winds of Winter (mentioned)

Urrigon Greyjoy,[4] affectionately called Urri by his brother Aeron,[1] was the fourth son of Lord Quellon Greyjoy and Lady Sunderly. He died in his childhood from an infection.[1]

In a sample chapter for The Winds of Winter, he is called Urrathon Greyjoy.[5]

Contents

  • 1 History
  • 2 Recent Events
    • 2.1 The Winds of Winter
  • 3 Family
  • 4 References

History[ ]

Urrigon and his younger brother Aeron were great friends during their childhood.[1] They were both sexually abused by their brother Euron as children.[5] Urrigon and Aeron often played at the finger dance together. Once, Aeron threw an axe that shaved off part of Urrigon's hand. The maester of their father's new wife, Lady Piper, insisted on sewing Urrigon's fingers back on instead of treating the amputation. Urrigon's hand became infected. They eventually amputated his arm, but it was already too late, and Urrigon died at the age of fourteen as a result of the infection.[1]

Urrigon's father, Lord Quellon Greyjoy, and older brothers, Balon, Euron, and Victarion, had been away from Pyke at the time, fighting the final battles of Robert's Rebellion. Lord Quellon died, and Urrigon's brother Balon returned home as the new Lord of the Iron Islands. To avenge his brother's death, he took off the maester's fingers and had Lady Piper sew them back on. The maester died raving.[1]

Recent Events[ ]

The Winds of Winter[ ]

 Warning
This information has thus far been released in a sample chapter for The Winds of Winter, and might therefore not be in finalized form. Keep in mind that the content as described below is still subject to change.

When Aeron Greyjoy is captive and chained in the hold of the Silence, his brother Euron forces him to drink shade-of-the-evening, which causes him to have numerous nightmarish visions. One of them features Urrathon with his arm infested with maggots.[5]
